executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations victimized on April 29, 2026—the highest single-day count on record. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (fashion retailer), National Money Mart (financial services with confirmed database leak), Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Eastern Townships School Board, and multiple healthcare providers. The Qilin ransomware group alone claims 23 Canadian victims in 24 hours, followed by Clop (14), Play (11), and SafePay (11). Critical infrastructure sectors hit hardest include healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and manufacturing—suggesting coordinated or opportunistic mass exploitation.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 23 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Bergmanis Preyra (law firm), Pre-Con Builders, Questica, Eastern Townships School Board, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a catastrophic ransomware wave on April 29, 2026, with 103 victim organizations reported across all provinces and sectors. Critical incidents include public health (Peterborough Public Health), municipal government (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Bais Yaakov school), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), and healthcare providers. No CCCS advisories were issued in the 48-hour reporting window despite this historic attack volume.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (fashion retail) confirmed victim of CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) hit by Sinobi ransomware.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan provider) breached by Everest ransomware with database confirmed leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi; Trucash financial services victimized by Lynx; multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Reidhurst Nagy) also compromised. No major bank (Big 5) incidents reported.

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA added 4 actively exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including CVE-2025-29635 being weaponized by Mirai botnets against end-of-life D-Link routers. Canadian Critical Infrastructure Advisory: CCCS issued AL25-012 warning of critical Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363). High-profile breaches: ShinyHunters claims 10 million ADT customer records stolen via vishing, plus 1.4 million Udemy records; Citizens Bank faces lawsuits following Everest ransomware attack. Canada-specific threat: Toronto police seized never-before-seen 'SMS blasters' that caused 13 million network disruptions and compromised tens of thousands of devices, blocking 911 calls.

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian Cyber Centre (CCCS) issued critical advisory AL25-012 for Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities requiring immediate patching. Toronto police made first-ever arrests linked to mobile SMS blasters—vehicle-mounted devices that caused 13 million network disruptions, infiltrated tens of thousands of devices, and blocked emergency 911 calls across Canadian streets. This represents a novel cybercrime threat never before seen in Canada.
canada_landscape.financial: Citizens Bank suffered data breach following Everest ransomware attack and third-party compromise, now facing class action lawsuits. Note: Citizens Bank is U.S.-based but included for financial sector context.
